Empowering Youth.

I'm JOSHUA YAOTEY — an aspiring entrepreneur on a mission to bridge the gap between young people and the world of work. Through the Youth Skills & Employment Hub (YSEH), I am building a platform that equips youth with practical, real-world skills and connects them directly to employment opportunities across Ghana and beyond.

Explore My Journey →
Scroll to discover
Chapter 01

My Entrepreneurial Journey

Every journey starts with a question. Mine started with this one: "Why are so many capable young people in my community unemployed?" That question set me on a path of observation, research, and eventually, action. Below is the story of how I went from asking questions to building solutions.

The Beginning — Noticing the Problem

A Gap I Could Not Ignore

Growing up, I observed a troubling pattern around me — talented, hardworking young people finishing school yet struggling to find employment. They had certificates, but lacked the practical skills that employers actually demanded. Graduates were applying for jobs without portfolios, technical know-how, or professional experience. This disconnect between education and employment deeply frustrated me and planted the first seed of YSEH.

Phase Two — Research & Exploration

Understanding the Landscape

I began researching the scale of youth unemployment in Ghana and across Africa. I spoke with students, recent graduates, and employers to understand the real barriers from multiple perspectives. What I found was consistent: employers wanted candidates with hands-on experience, digital literacy, and soft skills — yet formal education was delivering very little of these. I also researched existing solutions and found them either too expensive, too inaccessible, or not designed with Ghanaian youth in mind.

Phase Three — Concept Development

From Insight to Idea

Armed with my research, I began developing the concept for the Youth Skills & Employment Hub. The idea was to create a centralised, accessible platform that would offer practical skills training, mentorship, and direct links to employers — all tailored specifically for young people in Ghana. I began sketching early models, identifying key features, and seeking feedback from peers, teachers, and community members to refine the concept.

Now — Building the Foundation

Turning Vision into Reality

Today, I am actively developing the prototype for YSEH and presenting it as a viable entrepreneurial venture. I have defined the platform's core features, mapped out the user journey, and identified the key partners — training providers, employers, and community organisations — needed to make it work. This is the beginning of something I believe can transform employment outcomes for thousands of young Ghanaians.

Challenges Along the Way

Building something new is rarely straightforward. Here are the key challenges I encountered and what they taught me:

⚡

Limited Access to Resources

As a young entrepreneur without funding or an established network, I had to be resourceful — leveraging free tools, peer collaboration, and the guidance of teachers to move my idea forward.

🔍

Validating a Complex Problem

Youth unemployment is a multi-layered issue. Defining which specific part of the problem YSEH would solve — and proving that my solution was viable — required extensive research and iteration.

🤝

Being Taken Seriously

As a student entrepreneur, convincing others that a young person could meaningfully address a national challenge required confidence, evidence, and persistence — all of which I developed through this process.

Chapter 02

The Solution: YSEH

The Youth Skills & Employment Hub (YSEH) is a digital and community-based platform designed to equip young people — particularly those aged 15–30 in Ghana — with practical, employer-valued skills and to connect them directly with job opportunities and mentors.

YSEH addresses the critical gap between what schools teach and what employers actually need. Unlike generic online learning platforms, YSEH is built specifically for the Ghanaian and African youth context: it is affordable, locally relevant, mobile-friendly, and tied directly to real employment outcomes.

The platform benefits three groups simultaneously: youth who gain skills and job access, employers who gain a pipeline of work-ready candidates, and communities that see reduced unemployment and increased economic participation.

  • Practical skills courses (digital literacy, financial literacy, vocational trades)
  • Mentorship matching — connecting youth with experienced professionals
  • Employer portal — businesses post opportunities, youth apply directly
  • Mobile-first design accessible on low-cost smartphones
  • Community hubs — physical access points for youth without internet at home
  • Progress tracking and certification to build verifiable credentials
33%
Youth unemployment rate in Ghana (aged 15–24)
3.8M+
Young Ghanaians entering the labour market each year
1 Hub
One platform. Skills + mentorship + employment — all in one place.
Chapter 03

The Prototype

An idea only becomes entrepreneurship when it takes a tangible form. The YSEH prototype represents the first working model of the platform — a proof of concept that demonstrates how the solution would function for real users. Below is an overview of the prototype, how it works, and what it looks like.

How YSEH Works — Step by Step

A walkthrough of the user experience from first visit to employment outcome:

01

Youth Registers & Profiles

A young person signs up on the YSEH platform, creates a profile, and completes a short skills assessment to identify their interests, current abilities, and career goals.

02

Selects a Skills Pathway

Based on their assessment, the platform recommends personalised learning pathways — from digital marketing to carpentry to financial literacy — delivered through short, practical modules.

03

Connects with a Mentor

YSEH matches each user with a vetted professional mentor in their chosen field. Mentors provide guidance, accountability, and real-world industry insight through regular check-ins.

04

Applies for Opportunities

Upon completing a pathway, youth gain a YSEH credential and are connected to relevant job listings, apprenticeships, and internships posted by partner employers on the platform.

Prototype Screens

Current stage: The YSEH prototype is currently a conceptual wireframe and low-fidelity mockup. The next iteration will involve building a functional MVP using a no-code platform, conducting user testing with a small group of youth participants, and onboarding the first employer partners to validate the two-sided marketplace model.

Chapter 04

Reflection

"I did not start this journey because I knew all the answers. I started because I was tired of accepting the problem as inevitable."

Entrepreneurship is not just about building products — it is about building yourself. This journey stretched me intellectually, emotionally, and creatively. Looking back, I can see how much I have grown — not just as an entrepreneur, but as a thinker and a leader.

Key Lessons from My Journey

01

Start Before You're Ready

I waited a long time for the "perfect" plan before I started. I eventually realised that action — even imperfect action — teaches more than planning ever will. Getting started unlocked momentum I never had while waiting.

02

Failure is Information

Early assumptions about what youth needed turned out to be wrong. Rather than feeling discouraged, I learned to treat every failed assumption as data that brought me closer to a solution that genuinely worked.

03

Listen More Than You Speak

The most valuable insights about YSEH did not come from my own thinking — they came from listening deeply to the youth and employers I was trying to serve. Real entrepreneurship is about solving their problem, not your assumption of it.

04

Purpose Fuels Persistence

There were moments of doubt and exhaustion. What kept me going was always returning to my "why" — the image of a young person in my community finding work, gaining skills, and building a future. Purpose is the most renewable source of energy an entrepreneur has.

The biggest mindset shift I experienced was moving from thinking "I need to have all the answers before I begin" to understanding that "the answers reveal themselves through the doing." This shift was catalysed by studying real entrepreneurs who built great companies not from perfect plans, but from persistent iteration and a genuine desire to create value for others.

If I could give one piece of advice to my past self at the start of this journey it would be: "Trust your observations. The problem you see every day is exactly the problem worth solving."

Chapter 05

The Road Ahead

This entrepreneurial journey has been one of the most formative experiences of my life. What began as a question — "why are talented young people struggling to find work?" — has evolved into a structured, well-researched, and prototype-backed solution: the Youth Skills & Employment Hub (YSEH).

YSEH addresses a real, pressing, and urgent need. Youth unemployment in Ghana is not a small problem — it affects millions of young people, their families, and the economic future of the nation. By equipping youth with practical skills, mentorship, and direct employer connections, YSEH has the potential to meaningfully shift those numbers.

Through this journey, I have grown from someone who noticed a problem into someone who is actively building a solution. I have developed skills in research, ideation, prototyping, and entrepreneurial thinking that will serve me long beyond this project.

The prototype is just the beginning. The vision is much larger: a fully operational platform reaching youth across Ghana, and eventually across Africa — one practical skill and one employment opportunity at a time.

"Africa's greatest resource is its young people. My purpose is to make sure they have every tool they need to prove it."

— JOSHUA YAOTEY, Founder, YSEH

Next Steps

👥 Pilot with 20 youth users

📊 Measure outcomes
